title: Chapter Fourteen: Foundations
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Baek Mi-ryeo's face went blank.

It was an expression hard to imagine on her -- the cold, bold swordswoman famous even outside Desolate Sect. She stood still, fingers tracing her lips, thinking.

"Internal strength. Inner qi cultivation."

She did not get angry. That was something.

"It is hard to accept," she said at last. "I am an internal martial arts master. I have spent years cultivating inner energy. Do I look like a fool who cannot grasp her own true qi?"

*You do not look like a fool. But it appears to be true.*

He could not say that aloud.

From what he could see, her qi circulation was gentle, her true qi mobilisation faster than other seniors. But if she did not realise it herself, pressing the point would accomplish nothing.

"I am sorry," Yeon-shin said. "Unnecessary words."

He changed the subject by not having one. He turned to leave.

The plainness of his retreat was worse than insistence. Baek Mi-ryeo's brow furrowed -- an experienced master reading his withdrawal for what it was.

"I will be going now."

"Alright. I am sorry. And thank you."

He walked toward the small training ground.

*If she does not accept it, that is enough. If she holds a grudge, she is no different from the Jeong brothers. There is no reason to waste energy on such a person.*

He pushed Baek Mi-ryeo from his thoughts. Picked up his sword.

*Rotational strike combination techniques. Let us begin.*

---

"Chief."

"Speak."

"Did you tell Lightning Flash about the origins of my martial arts?"

"What nonsense. Do you think that makes sense?"

In Radiant Demon Wing's quarters, behind a sound-blocking qi barrier, Ma Jin frowned. Baek Mi-ryeo sat across from him, her face serious.

"Then how --"

"I can guess what happened." Ma Jin leaned back. "He saw through whatever technique you have been tinkering with. That boy is special, Mi-ryeo. A talent that is hard to find in the entire martial world. Famous clans, iron clans, absolute masters said to be the strongest in the world -- they all had moments of transcendence. Lightning Flash simply possesses something unfathomable."

Silence.

"Baek Mi-ryeo of the One Sword School, secretly training in the lost Heavenly Demon Divine Technique -- that is no reason to distance yourself from him. He is the future of Desolate Sect. Whatever advice he gave you, do not dismiss it as counsel from a lesser-skilled boy. I have never encountered such talent."

"I know that."

"If there is anything concerning, it is his personality. A small mission has come down. One blue-rank could handle it alone, but I am sending two blues and two whites. Go. Observe how Lightning Flash crosses the line of life and death. See if he has a trustworthy character."

"Are you saying I should discuss the Heavenly Demon Divine Technique with him if his personality is sound?"

"Your self-study has limits. I do not have the time. If we are talking about training in the Divine Technique, Lightning Flash's help would be worth more than mine. He adapts everything to suit himself. He has the qualities of a great founder."

"Great founder!"

Her eyes went wide. Ma Jin continued without raising his voice.

"You need not worry about orthodoxy. He will create his own martial arts lineage. You can see it in the Radiant Demon Formation -- I have never seen anyone train it the way he does."

"That is surprising."

"Surprising? The word is insufficient. Moreover, the boy enjoys martial arts study the way other boys enjoy food or play. He finds joy in it."

A long breath from Baek Mi-ryeo. She sat with her hands on her knees, staring at the floor. The weight of what Ma Jin was saying settled on her shoulders -- not the Heavenly Demon Divine Technique, not the question of orthodoxy, but the sheer magnitude of the boy's potential.

She had watched him spar. Had felt his knee strike crack through her guard. Had seen in his eyes that quiet, relentless hunger -- not for power, but for understanding.

*He is not like the rest of us. He does not merely practise martial arts. He dismantles them and rebuilds them from the ground up.*

"Within five years," Ma Jin said, "the Radiant Demon Wing Commander position will change to Lightning Flash. Go observe the personality of the one who will become your superior."

---

The next day.

Yeon-shin and Heon Won-chang were called to the main training ground. Three others waited: Baek Mi-ryeo, Cheong Myeong, and Ma Jin.

"It is a mission."

Heon Won-chang's body trembled. "Finally. The martial world --"

"Do not get excited. You might die out there."

"Yes, Chief!"

"Baek Mi-ryeo. Cheong Myeong. Jeong Yeon-shin. Heon Won-chang. The four of you will go. It is a matter entangled with the Blood Flame Cult. Leave Desolate Sect with determination."

Blood Flame Cult.

The words landed on Yeon-shin like cold water. A burning manor. Blood dripping from the doorstep. Red clothes and red hair.

He gripped his sword hilt.

"Are you saying we are sending two blues and two whites for a matter involving the Thirteen Demons?"

"Strictly speaking, it is not the Blood Flame Cult's main branch. We do not even know where that is." Ma Jin laid out the situation. "You know the famine is not limited to Huguang."

Yeon-shin did. While Xinye County had been passable, the broader region had suffered. Arable land shrinking across Henan. Shaanxi the same.

"A few years ago, starving commoners looted the manor of a village elder in Zhenping County, southern Shaanxi. Recently, the eldest son returned. His hair had turned red. He wore bright red martial robes."

"He joined the Blood Flame Cult," Heon Won-chang breathed.

"He gained their blood techniques quickly. He has settled near the village, periodically slaughtering livestock and ruining crops. He does not kill everything -- does not dig up the seeds, does not poison the wells. He waits until they grow back and does it again."

"Is that not merciful?" Heon Won-chang blurted. "For the Blood Flame Cult, I mean. The ones I have heard about are --"

"Merciful?" Ma Jin's voice was flat as a blade. "Are you serious?"

Heon Won-chang's face went red. Baek Mi-ryeo looked at him with an expression of open contempt.

"He is making them suffer in perpetuity," Yeon-shin said quietly. "Killing the harvest but leaving the farmers alive to watch. Slaughtering the livestock but allowing the herd to replenish. It is not mercy. It is a cage built from hope."

Ma Jin nodded. "Exactly. It is cruelty of the most deliberate kind."

"The Shaanxi branch could handle it," Ma Jin continued, "but Mount Hua Sect's Purple Shao Pill was recently stolen. The entire Shaanxi martial world is in uproar. They do not have the capacity. They are asking us."

He looked at them. At Heon Won-chang, whose eyes were too bright. At Yeon-shin, whose eyes were too still.

"Cheong Myeong. Baek Mi-ryeo. Take care of the whites. Bring them back alive."

"Aye."

"I will do my best."

Yeon-shin stroked his sword hilt. Behind the still-face, behind the door he kept closed, something stirred.

*If we meet them, I will kill them.*

*Keep your heads safe. I will cut them off.*

---
